location: This is Shadow of the Erdtree DLC content obtained through Moore's questline in Scadu Altus, not base-game content. Depending on choices made with Moore: (a) loot his corpse on the cliffs north of the Church of the Crusade if told to 'remain sad forever'; (b) loot his corpse after he joins the fight against Leda and her allies near Enir-Ilim if instead told to 'put it behind you'; or (c) kill the Rot Pest lying on the cliff north of the Church of the Crusade before progressing his questline at all, which triggers Moore to appear early as a hostile invader dropping the shield but ends his questline prematurely.
tactics: This weapon is DLC-exclusive — it cannot be obtained in a base-game-only playthrough despite being catalogued alongside base-game greatshields.

Killing the Rot Pest near the Church of the Crusade early is the fastest route to the shield but permanently derails the rest of Moore's questline — only do this if you don't care about seeing it through.
If you want the full questline, the two dialogue branches with Moore both eventually make the shield obtainable, just from different corpses/encounters later on.
lore: Forged from a strange, greenish metal called verdigris, this shield trades away some raw damage negation for the highest guard capacity of any greatshield in the game — built so sturdily, it's said, that it goes on shielding its wielder even after they've fallen.

builds.name: Ultimate STR turtle
builds.why: Highest guard boost of any greatshield in the game (82, rising to 90 at max upgrade) rewards a maxed Strength tank build willing to carry its 28.0 weight and meet its 49 Strength requirement.
